sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the Immigration and Deportation Short title
sections.text:
This Act may be cited as the Immigration and Deportation Short title
(Amendment) Act, 2016, and shall be read as one with the
Immigration and Deportation Act, 2010, in this Act referred to as Act No. 18
of 2010
the principal Act.

sections.heading: Section two of the principal Act is amended by the deletion Amendment
sections.text:
Section two of the principal Act is amended by the deletion Amendment
of section 2
of the definition of “child” and the substitution therefor of the
following:
“child” has the meaning assigned to it in the Constitution;. Cap. 1

sections.heading: Section three of the principal Act is amended in subsection Amendment
sections.text:
Section three of the principal Act is amended in subsection Amendment
(1) by the deletion of paragraph (c) . of section 3

sections.heading: Section four of the principal Act is amended by the deletion Amendment
sections.text:
Section four of the principal Act is amended by the deletion Amendment
of the words “Public Service Commission”, wherever they appear, of section 4
and the substitution therefor of the words “Zambia Police Service
Commission”.

sections.heading: Section eighteen of the principal Act is amended in Amendment
sections.text:
Section eighteen of the principal Act is amended in Amendment
subsection (1) (a) by the deletion, immediately after the word, of section 18

Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer,
P.O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka, Price K8.00 each.
Immigration and Deportation

Amendment 7. Section twenty of the principal Act is amended—
of section 20

Amendment 10. Section twenty-eight of the principal Act is amended by—
of section 28
(a) the deletion of subsection (2); and
(b) the deletion in subsection (5) of the word “five” and the
substitution therefor of the word “ten”.
Amendment 11. Section forty of the principal Act is amended in subsection
of section 40
(2) by the deletion of the word “deportation” and the substitution

Amendment 12. Section fifty-two of the principal Act is amended by the
of section 52
deletion of subsection (5) and the substitution therefor of the
following:
(5) A person who attempts to use or uses a passport,
diplomatic passport or travel document, other than the one
upon which that person’s entry was cleared, commits an
offence.
(6) Subsection (5) does not apply to—
(a) a person whose passport, diplomatic passport or
travel document has been lost or stolen and in
respect of which a police report has been made
and is produced at the time of clearance proving
the loss or theft; or
(b) a citizen of Zambia who holds dual citizenship.
Immigration and Deportation
(Amendment) [No. 19 of 2016 509

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the insertion, immediately Insertion of
sections.text:
The principal Act is amended by the insertion, immediately Insertion of
section 56A
after section fifty-six, of the following new section:
56A. (1) An immigration officer may, where satisfied Payment of
that a person has committed an offence for which the penalty fine without
appearing in
does not exceed two hundred thousand penalty units, or where court
a person has admitted the commission of an offence under
this Act for which the penalty does not exceed two hundred
thousand penalty units, summarily demand from the person
the payment of a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand
penalty units in respect of the offence.
(2) An immigration officer shall, where the immigration
officer demands a payment under subsection (1), inform the
person against whom the demand is made of the right to admit
or dispute the liability.
(3) A person from whom payment of a fine has been
demanded under subsection (1) may elect to admit liability
and pay the fine or dispute liability.
(4) The payment of a fine shall operate as a bar to any
further criminal proceedings against the person making the
payment in respect of the offence concerned.
(5) An immigration officer who receives payment of a
fine shall give a receipt to the person making the payment in
such form as may be prescribed.
(6) An immigration officer who receives payment under
this section and—
(a) fails to issue a receipt to the person making the
payment as required under subsection (5);
(b) fails to account for any payment made under this
section; or
(c) in any manner, misuses or puts to personal use any
payment made under this section;
commits an offence and is liable, upon conviction, to a fine
not exceeding three hundred thousand penalty units or to
imprisonment for a period not exceeding three years, or to
both.

sections.heading: The First Schedule to the principal Act is amended in class Amendment
sections.text:
The First Schedule to the principal Act is amended in class Amendment
C by the insertion of the word “spouse” and a comma between the of First
Schedule
words “any” and “forebear.”
